rationale:
This revision faithfully implements v12 Task 2 within frozen scope and correctly applies both of The Eye's rulings plus the prior review's fixes. Verified point by point against the frozen bundle:

RULING (A) — edge id-vs-locator validation: validateEdgeInput now requires the exact signed-edge payload field set (edge_kind, from_node, to_node, from_locator, to_locator, source_ref, asserted_by, assertion_status), validates from_locator/to_locator as exact {kind, locator} descriptors, requires stated ids to be lowercase 64-hex, derives ids via deriveNodeId, and REJECTS mismatches with distinct from_node/to_node errors — never silently replacing the stated id. The endpoint matrix is applied to the locator kinds. Tests cover both mismatch directions plus a non-64-hex stated id.

RULING (B) — private fixture-only git allowlist: the test builds its own no-shell (execFileSync with shell:false, argument arrays) allowlist permitting exactly init/config/add/commit/rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD/rev-parse --is-shallow-repository/clone -q [--depth 1] <url> <dest>. It lives entirely in the test, is not exported, is not the Task 4a weaver-facing wrapper, and the allowlist itself is negatively tested (status and rev-parse HEAD rejected). This matches D18's letter given Task 4a's wrapper does not yet exist; the plan's 'existing no-shell wrapper' phrasing cannot bind Task 2 to a Task 4a deliverable, and The Eye ruled accordingly.

Prior-review fixes confirmed: forEach passes the facade (not the private set) as the third callback argument, with a test asserting identity and immutability of that argument; WEAVER_IDS and ShallowRepositoryError are module-private (import list in the test confirms only the 12 frozen interface names are consumed); deriveNodeId and docAddressKey enforce exact outer schemas via requireExactKeys with tests for extra fields; isModelAssertor requires a nonempty seat ('model:' alone is rejected, tested); deriveRepositoryRef accepts only exact output forms — stripTerminalNewline removes at most one terminal line ending, 'false ' (trailing space) is malformed, 'yes' is malformed-and-distinct-from-shallow (tested with a predicate excluding the shallow code), non-string runner output is fatal, multi-line rev-list is fatal, non-40-hex root is fatal.

Frozen-scope conformance: all 12 interface names exported, exactly; registries are read-only facades over private Sets (not Object.freeze(new Set)) with size/has/iteration/keys/values/entries/forEach and throwing add/delete/clear; membership lists match the spec exactly (11/8/5, all literals verified in tests with counts and iteration); canonicalJson implements the exact acceptance/rejection rules (undefined, sparse arrays, non-finite, bigint, symbol, function, cycles, non-plain prototypes; code-unit key sort; array order preserved); all eleven per-kind locator schemas match the table including the commit exception (no repository_ref — extra-field test confirms), content bindings per D13 (blob_sha/text_sha256/entry_hash/summary_sha256 each validated per kind in a table-driven loop covering missing repository_ref, missing/short/uppercase content hash), heading normalization (NFC/trim/collapse), run-evidence docs/runs/ prefix, check-contract nonblank contract_id, canonical-path rejections (traversal, absolute, trailing slash, backslash, empty/./.. segments, NUL); requireExactKeys is genuinely exact (own-property-names sweep, symbol keys, own-enumerable requirement preventing canonicalJson omission collisions, inherited-enumerable rejection with a prototype-pollution test); source refs accept exactly the three forms with strict hex and path validation; status/assertor coupling matches the table exactly with an exhaustive category x status matrix test; endpoint matrix matches spec v2.3 exactly including all four depends-on rows and the exact discharges rows (code-symbol->obligation, obligation->contract-clause; reverse rejected); supersedes enforces same-kind + human/model:<seat> assertor with rename and content-version pair tests; deriveRepositoryRef implements the D16 sequence (shallow guard first with stable coded error CLOTHO_SHALLOW_REPOSITORY, then single-root 40-hex requirement) with injected units covering happy path, shallow, malformed-distinct-from-shallow, multi-root, malformed root, and non-string outputs; the D18 real-git fixture builds a multi-commit origin, proves --depth 1 file:// clone throws the shallow error and full clone resolves exactly git-root:<origin root>, cleans up in finally, and runs in the normal suite.

Hygiene: zero runtime dependencies, ESM, node: stdlib only (node:crypto in the module; node: imports only in the test), no spine change (deterministic gate confirms diff confined to clotho/), no scope expansion (nothing beyond the 12 interfaces exported; edge id-vs-locator validation is within the frozen validateEdgeInput surface per ruling A). The deterministic gate passed all checks including test-all green.

Minor observations, none blocking: (1) the injected-git happy-path alternate runner returns bare strings without newlines — acceptable since real git output with one terminal newline is also covered; (2) requireHex's error label derives from regex identity, fine for the two fixed regexes used; (3) the fixture's config/add argument-count checks are loose within the allowlist (e.g., any 3-arg config), but the allowlist is fixture-private, no-shell, and negatively tested, satisfying ruling B's intent — tightening would be cosmetic. I stake my seat's signature on this approval.
